title: 2 men arrested after fatal overdose on Cape Cod prompts monthslong, multi-town investigation
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: William Sarnie and Andre Silvia were arrested for allegedly trafficking over 200 grams of cocaine.
The arrests followed a monthslong investigation initiated by a fatal overdose in Barnstable County in November 2025.
Authorities conducted extensive searches involving local and state police across Barnstable, Nantucket, Sandwich, and Wareham.

title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Harry Styles hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Harry Styles hosted and performed as the musical guest on the March 14, 2026, episode of 'Saturday Night Live.'
Ryan Gosling introduced Styles' first musical performance, 'Dance No More,' in a guest appearance on the show.
This marks Styles' second time hosting SNL and his seventh overall appearance, including past performances with One Direction.

title: Former Boston Public Schools teacher sentenced to 10 years in prison for child exploitation
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: John Magee Gavin, 35, a former Boston Public Schools teacher, was sentenced to 10 years for child exploitation.
Gavin pleaded guilty in December 2025 to coercion, enticement of a minor, and possession and receipt of child pornography.
Authorities discovered Gavin's Discord account used to exploit at least 20 underage girls across the U.S., Canada, and the UK.

title: 5 unsigned free agents that would be possible fits for Patriots
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Patriots explore signing Tyreek Hill if his legal and injury issues resolve, adding depth to their receiver room.
Marquise Brown, a speed receiver, is considered as a potential addition to augment Patriots' receiving options.
Linebacker Bobby Okereke is a top free agent target to solidify New England's uncertain linebacker group.

title: Neemias Queta dominates in Celtics win over Wizards: 7 takeaways
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Neemias Queta scored 24 points and secured 10 rebounds in the Celtics' 111-100 win over the Wizards.
Jayson Tatum contributed 20 points and 14 rebounds, but struggled with his 3-point shooting, going 2 for 9.
Payton Pritchard had a difficult game, shooting 2 for 11 from the field with no free throw attempts.

title: Photos: Highlights from Dropkick Murphys’ St. Patrick’s Day kickoff at MGM Music Hall
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Dropkick Murphys kicked off St. Patrick’s Day weekend with a show at MGM Music Hall on March 13.
The concert featured performances by opening acts The Vigilantes, Haywire, and Showcase Showdown.
Dropkick Murphys' set included performances by Ken Casey, Kevin Rheault, Jeff DaRosa, and Tim Brennan.

title: ‘Feisty I think is the word’: The Boston vs. New York rivalry arrives in NWSL for Legacy FC’s debut
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC debuted in NWSL with a 1-0 loss to New York's Gotham FC, witnessed by over 30,000 fans.
The match saw an NWSL record-tying eight yellow cards, with Legacy's Bianca St-Georges receiving a red card.
Both teams emphasized the rivalry's intensity, with coaches noting the game's physicality and aggressive play.

title: Review and setlist: Dropkick Murphys fires up crowd, flames ICE at St. Paddy’s Day kickoff show
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Dropkick Murphys performed at MGM Music Hall at Fenway on March 13, 2026, kicking off a series of St. Patrick's Day shows.
The band debuted 'Only the Strong' from their upcoming EP 'New England Forever' during the encore of their high-energy set.
Ken Casey addressed political issues, notably condemning ICE, and paid tribute to immigrants during the concert.

title: He’s running Boston to support people like him with Type 1 diabetes
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Collin Bennett, 29, from New Braintree, Mass., runs Boston Marathon for Team Beth Israel Lahey Health.
Bennett was diagnosed with Type 1 diabetes at 11, supported by Joslin Diabetes Center throughout his journey.
He runs to inspire others with Type 1 diabetes and raise awareness for a cure.

title: Springfield native Ruth E. Carter keeps making Oscars history
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Ruth E. Carter, from Springfield, becomes most-nominated Black woman in Oscar history with her fifth nomination.
In 2019, Carter was the first Black person to win the Oscar for Best Costume Design.
Carter's recent Oscar nomination is for her work on the film 'Sinners,' featuring a Massachusetts clothing connection.
